message:
So, the other day, I was in the kitchen making dinner while my kids were engaged in their usual chaos—playing tag, arguing over whose turn it was for the remote, you know the drill. Suddenly, I caught a whiff of something burning. My heart raced as I turned to see the pan I had forgotten on the stove, smoke curling up like it was auditioning for a role in a disaster movie! 

In that moment, I realized I hadn’t talked to the kids about kitchen safety in a while. After a quick rescue mission to salvage dinner (and my pride!), we sat down and had a family chat about fire safety, including the importance of smoke alarms and having an escape plan. 

It was a good reminder that while I try to prepare for everything, sometimes the simplest conversations can make the biggest difference. I even found a little refresher that helped spark our discussion! 

Have you had any kitchen mishaps that turned into teaching moments?
